decarb_plan.measure: LED lighting + smart thermostat retrofit — common areas & units
decarb_plan.incentive_program: Xcel Energy CO — Multifamily Efficiency rebate

decarb_plan.measure: Central DHW electrification — heat pump water heater plant replacing gas boilers
decarb_plan.incentive_program: IRA §48E ITC — 30%; Fannie Mae Green Rewards

decarb_plan.measure: Roof-mounted solar PV (common-area offset), bundled with re-roof
Envelope air sealing + attic insulation top-up, bundled with re-roof
EV charging + resident sustainability amenity package
decarb_plan.incentive_program: Denver EV charging incentive; Xcel Energy EV rebate

deal_signal.narrative: Denver's Energize Denver ordinance sets a 2027 interim EUI target the property is ~14% above today — its 2006-vintage gas-fired DHW boiler plant is the primary driver. Closing the gap requires ~$675K ($2,720/unit) in electrification CapEx not reflected in the seller's capital plan; price this into the offer or negotiate a capital credit.

emissions_profile.fuel_profile: Dual-fuel — natural gas heat + central DHW; electric cooling and lighting
emissions_profile.utility_structure: Resident electric submetered; landlord pays common-area electric + all natural gas (central DHW boiler plant)
emissions_profile.baseline_emissions: ~46 kgCO2e/m2yr (est., dual-fuel garden multifamily benchmark, Denver climate zone 5B; independent sector benchmark, not derived from the landlord-boundary GHG scope table below, which excludes resident-metered load)
emissions_profile.crrem_pathway: ~19% above 2030 CRREM 1.5C target for US multifamily — Phase 2 DHW electrification + solar close most of the gap by Yr3
emissions_profile.regulation: Moderate-High — Energize Denver BPS: 2024 baseline reporting complete; 2027 interim EUI target at risk without DHW electrification; escalating per-sqft penalty begins 2027

hazards.finding: Denver metro sits in 'Hail Alley' — front-range severe-hail frequency is projected to increase, shortening roof and HVAC condenser replacement cycles.
South Platte basin allocation tightening; landscaping and common-area water costs trending up.
9 additional days above 95F by 2050 (NOAA); modest cooling-load increase for electric AC.
FEMA Zone X — outside the 500-year floodplain; minimal exposure.
Urban infill location, more than 8 miles from the nearest WUI boundary.

climate_var.primary_driver: Severe convective storm / hail — roof & building envelope
climate_var.methodology: physrisk NGFS/IPCC hazard overlay applied to asset value; EAL discounted over hold period

scopes.source: Natural gas — central DHW boiler plant
scopes.notes: Verified from utility schedule in the OM; excludes in-unit gas furnaces (resident-metered, Scope 3).

scopes.source: Grid electricity — common-area lighting, pumps, elevators
scopes.notes: Xcel Energy CO grid factor ~0.30 kgCO2/kWh.
Leased-space boundary — excluded from owner total per GHG Protocol Cat. 13 (Downstream Leased Assets).

ghg_scoping.offset_note: Annual REC offset cost for owner boundary (70 tCO2e): ~$1,050/yr.

certifications_and_debt.energy_star_recommendation: Pursue post-DHW retrofit — projected score ~68; benchmarking cost ~$6K one-time
certifications_and_debt.leed_recommendation: Not recommended — no fund LEED mandate; limited exit premium for garden-style suburban multifamily
certifications_and_debt.green_debt: Fannie Mae Green Rewards eligible — requires >=25% projected energy/water savings from Phase 1+2 measures
certifications_and_debt.fund_alignment: Phase 1+2 (DHW electrification + solar) aligns with Stonebridge Capital's 2030 portfolio decarbonization target (35% reduction)

seller_questions: Can you share 24 months of utility bills for the central gas DHW boiler plant and common-area electric — the OM does not include a utility schedule and this is the primary input for verifying our emissions baseline.
What is the age, service history, and remaining useful life of the two central DHW boilers? At 2006 vintage, boiler plants of this type are typically within 5 years of replacement.

Has the property filed any hail or wind-related insurance claims in the last 10 years, and has the current insurer signaled any non-renewal or deductible changes given Colorado's rising hail-loss trend?
Has the property submitted 2024 Energize Denver benchmarking data, and if so, what was the reported EUI and compliance path relative to the 2027 interim target?
Are there any existing green loan, PACE, or sustainability-linked financing covenants encumbering the property that would transfer to a buyer?

sources.label: Energize Denver BPS — interim EUI targets & penalty schedule
NOAA Climate Projections — Denver Front Range
Xcel Energy Colorado grid carbon intensity

data_quality: Medium — confirmed fuel profile; CapEx benchmarked
